scenario: Pulse Logistics, a logistics provider receiving 5000 shipment-status callbacks per day, fronts a Camunda 8.8 process with an HTTP Webhook connector on a plain start event (no message definition). The integration team needs each webhook response to return the freshly created process instance key to the caller with HTTP status 202 instead of the default 200.
question: Which Response expression returns the new processInstanceKey to the caller with a 202 status code?

options.text: ={"body": {"piKey": correlation.processInstanceKey}, "statusCode": 202} reading the new instance key from the correlation object on the plain start event
={"body": {"piKey": correlation.messageKey}, "statusCode": 202} reading the messageKey field from the correlation object returned by the start event
={"body": {"piKey": request.body.processInstanceKey}, "statusCode": 202} reading the key from the request body that the caller sent into the webhook
={"body": {"piKey": correlation.processInstanceKey}} relying on the default Response status code already being 202 for webhook start events that create instances

a.text: Correct. On a start event without a message, the correlation object exposes processInstanceKey and tenantId, and the Response expression sets the HTTP status with a statusCode key, so {body, statusCode: 202} returns the new instance key and overrides the default 200.

b.text: Incorrect. correlation.messageKey is populated only when the webhook fronts a start event with a message definition; on a plain start event the messageKey is absent and the correct property is processInstanceKey.

c.text: Incorrect. The new process instance key is generated by Zeebe at correlation time, so it is not present in request.body; the caller cannot know it before the webhook responds, so reading it from the request body yields nothing.

d.text: Incorrect. The default HTTP status of the Response expression is 200, not 202; without an explicit statusCode entry the connector returns 200 to the caller regardless of the BPMN element fronted by the webhook.

explanation: In the Response expression you can use the correlation object to access the result of correlation: a start event without a message exposes processInstanceKey and tenantId, while a message start event exposes messageKey and tenantId. The default HTTP status is 200, and you change it by including a statusCode key alongside body in the FEEL response context.
